# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    options {
        buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10'))
        disableConcurrentBuilds()
        timestamps()
    }

    parameters {
        string(name: 'BUILD_COMMAND', description: 'Custom Build Command')
        choice(name: 'TEST_SCRIPT', choices: ['JUnit', 'TestNG'], description: 'Select Test Script Type')
        string(name: 'DEPLOYMENT_STEP', description: 'Custom Deployment Step')
    }

    environment {
        GIT_BRANCH = env.BRANCH_NAME
        GIT_COMMIT = env.GIT_COMMIT
        BUILD_NUMBER = env.BUILD_NUMBER
    }

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh "${params.BUILD_COMMAND}"
            }
        }
        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                if (params.TEST_SCRIPT == 'JUnit') {
                    sh "mvn clean test"
                } else if (params.TEST_SCRIPT == 'TestNG') {
                    sh "mvn clean test -Dtestng.xml=your-testng-xml-path"
                }
            }
        }
        stage('Deploy') {
            steps {
                sh "${params.DEPLOYMENT_STEP}"
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            emailext body: 'Build failed',
                    subject: 'Jenkins Build Failed',
                    to: 'your-email@example.com'
        }
    }
}
